title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez responded to Greg Bedard's report questioning his physicality on social media.
Bedard claimed some Patriots coaches and scouts think Gonzalez isn't physical enough.
Gonzalez is viewed as a premier cornerback in the NFL and registered 69 tackles in 2025.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict Red Sox could pick Chase Brunson, an outfielder from TCU, with their 20th overall draft pick.
Analysts consider right-handed pitcher Taylor Rabe from Ole Miss as a potential first-round pick for the Red Sox.
Bo Lowrance, a high school third baseman from South Carolina, is among those anticipated to be selected by the Red Sox.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Long Walk,' a Stephen King adaptation, is now streaming on HBO Max.
'Project Hail Mary,' starring Ryan Gosling, is available on Prime Video.
'Little House on the Prairie' series is streaming on Netflix.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela was named to the 2026 MLB All-Star team, replacing injured Aaron Judge.
Rafaela joins Willson Contreras, Ranger Suarez, and Aroldis Chapman as Red Sox All-Stars.
Rafaela has a .283/.330/.438 slash line with 8 home runs and 39 RBIs through 87 games.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut will compete in the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship on July 11 at The Brook Casino, Seabrook.
Chestnut recently won his 18th Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest but faces Geoffrey Esper, a top wing eater, in Saugus Wings.
Saugus Wings are Kowloon's signature dish, and the event honors their significance in Massachusetts's North Shore.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in Boston to cap the city's successful hosting of the 2026 World Cup.
Kylian Mbappé scored, despite an ankle injury, leading France to victory alongside Ousmane Dembélé's goal.
Boston hosted seven matches and saw significant international fan engagement, boosting its global reputation.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola allegedly attacked her friend Marianna DiMercurio with a WD-40-soaked rag in Gloucester, MA.
Palazzola borrowed over $160,000 from DiMercurio and allegedly planned a murder-suicide to avoid repayment.
Palazzola faces charges including attempted murder and assault; arraignment is set for July 21.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Coco Fernandez, owner of Mobar in Waltham, became the go-to barber for France's World Cup soccer team.
Fernandez received a WhatsApp message leading to haircut sessions for Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé.
France's players, using Boston as their base, regularly use Fernandez's services during the tournament.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox faced a 17-hour travel delay from Chicago, affecting their series with the New York Mets.
The team's flight from Chicago was initially set for 9:45 p.m. Thursday, but left at 3 p.m. Friday.
Delays caused by multiple plane issues, including a broken tug and cockpit light, led to an overnight hotel stay.

title: Photos: See breathtaking tall ships sail toward Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels will arrive in Boston Harbor for the Sail Boston event starting July 11.
Sail Boston marks the final stop of the Sail250 tour, with ships arriving from New York and docking citywide.
The Five Sisters Race features German ship Gorch Folk and others, racing from New York to Boston by July 9.
